title: To The Most Comely And Proper M. Elizabeth Finch.
content:
Handsome you are, and proper you will be
    Despite of all your infortunity:
    Live long and lovely, but yet grow no less
    In that your own prefixed comeliness:
    Spend on that stock: and when your life must fall,
    Leave others beauty to set up withal.
meaning:
"To The Most Comely And Proper M. Elizabeth Finch." is a quintessential example of Robert Herrick's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
